name: flywheel
description: Standardize a compounding development flow from setup and ideation through execution, review, shipping, and learning capture.

interface.shortDescription: Compound engineering leverage across the dev loop
interface.longDescription: Guide work from repo setup, ideation, and measured optimization through brainstorming, planning, execution, review, shipping, and captured learnings so each completed task makes the next one easier.
defaultPrompt: Use $flywheel to take this task from idea to a working PR through the right Flywheel stage.
Use $fw-setup when you need to prove repo and machine readiness before starting.
Use $fw-run when one bounded task should be coordinated end to end through the remaining Flywheel stages.
Use $fw-brainstorm to refine an idea before planning.
Use $document-review to review a requirements doc or plan before execution.
Use $fw-deepen-plan when an existing plan needs stronger implementation units or verification before work starts.
Use $fw-plan to turn this request into an implementation plan.
Use $fw-debug when the immediate job is to prove a bug before fixing it.
Use $fw-browser-test when browser-visible behavior needs fresh acceptance proof.
Use $fw-polish when browser-visible behavior needs a live tighten-and-retest loop before review.
Use $fw-work to execute the plan, validate the implementation, and hand off cleanly into review.
Use $fw-optimize when the goal is measured latency, throughput, build, or cost improvement.
Use $fw-ship to finish the branch with commit, push, and PR closure.
Use $fw-spin when a completed session exposed durable lessons worth capturing for future work.
